ctxMarket: Attention is focused on real-world asset tokenization and security risks in DeFi, with notable institutional moves and major exploits shaping sentiment. Solana’s Q1 dominance is being challenged by Ethereum’s recent gains, especially in the DEX space.
ctxTalking:
• OCBC’s launch of a tokenized gold fund on both Ethereum and Solana
• A surge in DeFi exploits, including large-scale attacks on Drift and Kelp
• Shifting roles for Solana and Ethereum as AI agents drive new on-chain activity
ctxMatters: Tokenized real-world assets on public blockchains have surpassed $29 billion, signaling growing institutional interest. Security breaches have triggered significant outflows from DeFi, raising concerns about protocol resilience. The evolving use of AI agents may influence how Solana and Ethereum are positioned for future applications.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Institutional adoption and security incidents are directly impacting user confidence and ecosystem growth.
Noise: Short-term price rebounds tied to announcements without clear long-term effects.
ctxGlossary:
Tokenized asset — A real-world asset represented digitally on a blockchain
AI agent — Automated software executing tasks or trades on-chain
DEX — Decentralized exchange for peer-to-peer crypto trading

readMore.title: Singapore’s OCBC launches tokenized gold fund on Ethereum and Solana
Why AI agents may be pushing Solana and Ethereum into separate roles
North Korea’s crypto heist playbook is expanding and DeFi keeps getting hit
